metadata.title: Why asking Claude for JSON gives you prose — but a tool you never call gives you clean JSON
metadata.purpose: Tool invocation is a structurally distinct output channel from text; filling a schema forces conformance to field names and types instead of layering a formatting wish on top of free text.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro am_onyx) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.exclusions: tool_choice mechanics, API request structure, server-side parsing of tool_use
metadata.visual_object: Two stacked output blocks from one prompt — text lane (prose + red X) vs. tool_use lane (clean JSON + green check)
metadata.hook: "Return valid JSON" still ships a prose wrapper that crashes your parser; routing the same request through a tool schema you never execute makes the contamination vanish.

beats.narration_text: Prompt says "Return valid JSON" → Claude writes "Here is the JSON: {...}" → downstream `json.loads` throws. Define a `record_result` tool with the identical schema → Claude fills it → pure JSON arrives in the `tool_use` block → parser passes every call.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: Asking for JSON should produce JSON; routing output through a tool you don't need should not fix a formatting problem — why does it?
"Return valid JSON" still ships a prose wrapper that crashes your parser; routing the same request through a tool schema you never execute makes the contamination vanish.
Tool invocation is a structurally distinct output channel from text; filling a schema forces conformance to field names and types instead of layering a formatting wish on top of free text.
beats.visual: Two stacked output blocks from one prompt — text lane (prose + red X) vs. tool_use lane (clean JSON + green check)
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: "Classify this review, 0–1 confidence." Text lane: "I'd say **positive** (~0.8 confident)." Tool lane: `{"label":"positive","confidence":0.8}`. Parser: fail vs. pass.
Tool invocation is a structurally distinct output channel from text; filling a schema forces conformance to field names and types instead of layering a formatting wish on top of free text.
